question.text: Under the Depositories Act, which authority's powers over depositories do NOT extend to International Finance Service Centres (IFSCs)?

answer.text: The Central Government - the powers are exercised by the State Government instead
SAT - the powers are exercised by the High Court instead
RBI - the powers are exercised by the Ministry of Finance instead
SEBI - the powers are exercised by IFSCA instead

explanation.text: **The Logic:** As per IFSCA Act, 2019 read with Section 23G of the Depositories Act, the powers exercisable by SEBI under the Depositories Act shall not extend to IFSCs set up under Section 18(1) of the Special Economic Zones Act, 2005. These powers are exercised by the International Finance Services Centres Authority (IFSCA) established under section 4(1) of the IFSCA Act. **The Trap:** The carve-out is specifically for SEBI's powers, not for RBI or other regulators. IFSCA acts as the unified regulator for all financial services in IFSCs. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** IFSCA was established to provide a single unified regulator for IFSCs, replacing multiple domestic regulators (SEBI, RBI, IRDAI, PFRDA) within the IFSC jurisdiction.
